concerns.text: FR-006 states that predicted edges will be compared to STRING high‑confidence interactions (combined score ≥ 700). STRING’s combined score aggregates multiple evidence channels, including co‑expression derived from transcriptomic data. Because the predictor itself is built from co‑expression, the validation benchmark is not independent; it partially reflects the same signal the model uses, creating a circular validation that can inflate AUROC/AUPRC and undermines the claim that co‑expression alone recovers true PPIs.

SC-001 requires AUROC ≥ 0.70 against the same STRING combined‑score set. Without ensuring the benchmark excludes co‑expression evidence, the methodology cannot reliably assess the predictive value of co‑expression. The spec should either (a) use a STRING subset that omits the ‘co‑expression’ channel (e.g., only experimental, database, or text‑mining evidence), or (b) employ an orthogonal curated PPI resource such as BioGRID, IntAct, or DIP that is independent of expression data.
